channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Hungary’s Orbán interrupted by activist during EU presidency news conference
item.description: BUDAPEST, Hungary (AP) — An activist and municipal councilor for a Hungarian opposition party disrupted a news conference in the French city of Strasbourg on Tuesday, as Prime Minister Viktor Orbán was laying out his plans for Hungary's six-month presidency of the European Union.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump campaign blasts Woodward; denies secret Putin calls
item.description: Former President Trump's campaign is rejecting accounts in journalist Bob Woodward's new book that the Republican presidential nominee has held multiple phone calls with Russian President Vladimir Putin since leaving office in January 2021.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Former Taiwan leader Tsai to visit Czech Republic, other European destinations, drawing China's ire
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — Former Taiwan leader Tsai Ing-wen is to visit Czech Republic, and other European destinations, drawing China’s ire.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: German court acquits McCann suspect of unrelated sexual offense charges
item.description: BRAUNSCHWEIG, Germany (AP) — A German court on Tuesday acquitted a man who is also under investigation in the 2007 disappearance of British toddler Madeleine McCann in a trial on charges of unrelated sexual offenses.
item.dc:creator: FANNY BRODERSEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Water gushes through sand dunes after a rare rainfall in the Sahara desert
item.description: A rare deluge of rainfall has left lagoons of water amid the palm trees and sand dunes of the Sahara desert.
item.dc:creator: Sam Metz And Baba Ahmed, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Hezbollah steps up rocket fire as Israel sends more troops into Lebanon
item.description: Hezbollah has fired another barrage of rockets into Israel, and the militant group’s acting leader vows to keep up pressure that has forced thousands of Israelis from their homes near the Lebanese border.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: France’s minority government survives a no-confidence vote, 2 weeks after taking office
item.description: France’s minority government has survived a no-confidence vote, two weeks after taking office, getting over the first hurdle placed by left-wing lawmakers to bring down new conservative Prime Minister Michel Barnier.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Russian court sentences a 72-year-old American to nearly 7 years in prison for fighting in Ukraine
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — A Russian court on Monday sentenced a 72-year-old American in a closed trial to nearly seven years in prison for allegedly fighting as a mercenary in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mozambique's presidential election: Here's what to know
item.description: MAPUTO, Mozambique (AP) — Mozambicans are expected to turn out in large numbers on Wednesday for an election that will choose a successor to President Filipe Nyusi after his two terms.
item.dc:creator: CHARLES MANGWIRO and MOGOMOTSI MAGOME, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The mayor of a state capital in Mexico is killed less than 1 week after he took office
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Relatives on Monday buried the mayor of a state capital in southern Mexico who was killed just one week after he took office. Photos of the crime scene shared on social media showed Mayor Alejandro Arcos’ severed head had been left on the roof of a pickup truck.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Palestinian militants in Gaza fire rockets into Israel as it marks Oct. 7
item.description: Palestinian militants in Gaza fired a barrage of rockets into Israel on Monday as mourners marked the anniversary of the Oct. 7 attack, without disrupting a nearby ceremony.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former hostage wants ‘peace in the world’ one year later
item.description: One year later, the Chicago-area woman is steadfast in her faith and hopeful others captured that day will be released.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Reporter's Notebook: Iran’s missiles strike Israel, and a deadly terror attack in Tel Aviv
item.description: On the same day Iran attacked Israel with some 181 missiles last week, a terrorist attack saw 7 innocent Israelis murdered in cold blood by two Palestinians in the Tel Aviv area.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli military warns of operations on southern Lebanon's coast
item.description: Israel's military on Monday said it would soon launch operations on Lebanon’s southern coast, which would mark a further escalation of its fight against Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Mexican mayor murdered less than a week after taking office
item.description: Mayor Alejandro Arcos of Chilpancingo, Mexico, has been confirmed dead less than a week after being sworn into the notoriously dangerous city's highest office.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Albania's opposition protests and demands a caretaker Cabinet
item.description: TIRANA, Albania (AP) — Opposition supporters in Albania protested again Monday, demanding that the government be replaced by a technocratic caretaker Cabinet before next year’s parliamentary election.
item.dc:creator: LLAZAR SEMINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Four American hostages remain in Hamas captivity a year after Oct. 7 massacre
item.description: Secretary of State Antony Blinken is vowing that the U.S. "will continue to work tirelessly" to bring home Americans being held hostage by Hamas in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US hits Hamas funding network with sanctions on attack anniversary
item.description: The Treasury Department announced Monday, the anniversary of Hamas's Oct. 7 attack on Israel, it would be sanctioning part of the Palestinian militant group’s funding network.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Trump: Israel has to 'get smart' about supporting him
item.description: Former President Trump said Monday Israel has to “get smart” about supporting him and suggested Jewish people have not adequately backed him after he took several pro-Israel actions while in office.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Schumer: Oct. 7 'will go down in infamy'
item.description: Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer (D-N.Y.), the highest-ranking Jewish elected leader in American history, marked the first anniversary of the Oct. 7, 2023, attacks in Israel as a “day that will go down in infamy” and pledged America “will never forget.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, The Hill

item.title: Russian arms dealer Viktor Bout, who was traded for Brittney Griner, to sell weapons to Iran-backed Houthis
item.description: Russian "Merchant of Death" Viktor Bout has reportedly resumed his role as an arms dealer following his December 2022 release from U.S. custody.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: McConnell praises Israel’s 'unshakeable resolve' one year after Oct. 7 attacks
item.description: Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (R-Ky.) praised Israel’s “unshakeable resolve” to mark the first anniversary of the Oct. 7, 2023, attacks that left more than 1,100 people dead in Israeli communities outside of Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, The Hill

item.title: Iran’s chief of overseas arms dealings radio silent since Beirut strikes: Iranian officials
item.description: A top Iranian military leader who oversees dealings between Tehran and groups like Hezbollah has not been heard from since an attack that killed a Hezbollah official last week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran lifts flight restrictions ahead of schedule after grounding all flights for 'operational restrictions'
item.description: Flight restrictions were lifted in Iran after safe conditions were confirmed, state media reported on Monday, shortening the period of flight cancellations announced earlier.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Under fire: Israel's Iron Dome winning but pressure's on as conflict with Iran escalates
item.description: As the conflict in the Middle East intensifies, Iron Dome and Israel's other air defense systems have been continually challenged from prolonged use and evolving threats.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A strike on a mosque kills at least 19, Palestinian officials say, as Israel bombards northern Gaza and southern Lebanon
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — An Israeli strike on a mosque in the Gaza Strip early Sunday killed at least 19 people, Palestinian officials said, as Israel intensified its bombardment of northern Gaza and southern Lebanon in a widening war with Iran-allied militant groups across the region.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Boris Johnson alleges Netanyahu may have bugged his bathroom
item.description: Israel also was accused around the same time of planting listening devices in The White House, The Telegraph also reported.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Mexico’s new president promises to resume fight against climate change
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — In her first days as Mexico’s new president, Claudia Sheinbaum made a point of distancing herself from the fossil fuel reliance promoted by her predecessor and mentor, Andrés Manuel López Obrador, and vowed to resume an energy transition that he halted.
item.dc:creator: TERESA DE MIGUEL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Claudia Sheinbaum sworn in as 1st female president of Mexico
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Claudia Sheinbaum was sworn in Tuesday as Mexico’s first female president, riding the enthusiasm over her predecessor’s social programs but also facing challenges that include stubbornly high levels of violence.
item.dc:creator: FABIOLA SÁNCHEZ and MARIANA MARTÍNEZ BARBA, Associated Press, NewsNation
